module_mood_contract: curious readiness — 'I've built enough to try; let's see what the agent does' (M4 main exercise)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body line 23: 'The picking work is your judgement about what's been sitting; Claude's job is to check fit.' Matches strategy doc M4 move 1.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Four registry-expanded prompts ({{prompt:walk-and-send-off-1..4}}) carry the screening criteria, ranked-top-5, speed-up tool, and quote-first-name-second mechanics. Body prose frames, doesn't substitute.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Silent-failure naming — un-packaged-by-design struggle is the pedagogy.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 17 names the un-packaged contract as M5 teaching material; maintainer 'package-pre-empt' watch-for (line 153) prevents premature packaging.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 76 time-check callout uses 'to share what surfaced' without addressee; maintainer block line 105 declares this an accepted-by-design carve-out (invitational room-energy, not student-action mandate). Future judges instructed not to re-flag.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Offered structures need forced engagement (ranked-top-5 enforcement).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 2 prompt enforces ranked top-5; pacing note line 139 names re-run trigger when audit returns more.
Line 25 bolded: 'Not an epic. Not a typo-fix. A real slice you'd send off rather than nudge bit by bit, with a 'done' you can name in a sentence.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch named in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 123-128: six failure modes with diagnostics + fixes (task-sprawl, audit busywork, over-fill, Huryn-as-lecture, package-pre-empt, send-off anxiety).
Line 5: `**Session** *(new, "Module 4 - Run the first experiment")*` with /rename inside .session-block — exercise-top default pattern.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing doesn't preview prompt payoff.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 15 'What you do:' names activity + terminal artifact (pick task, walk, fill, see frame, compound, send off); no mechanism or felt-evidence preview. Line 91 'What happened:' at Phase 3 close (post-action position) — correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood-floor 8+ on teaching beats.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Trace records phase scores [8, 8, 8] and close 8; all phases are teaching beats (Phase 1 delegation judgement; Phase 2 sponge-not-rock; Phase 3 frame-by-recognition).

Lines 95-101 hand the send-off + self-compound to module Debrief, preserving M5's diagnosis material. Line 17 names the two-run-arc + un-packaged contract — Ronacher's three-pattern stays unnamed (M5 closer earns it).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Open-hook only where student input is genuinely required.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 1 prompt takes candidates 'after the colon, one per line' — open-hook supplies what Claude can't read cold (student's backlog).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers; minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise file carries no lectures. Huryn's three blocks earn the name through recognition in own material (Phase 3), not via pre-exercise lecture. Strategy doc Pre-read 'reading-the-return.md' lands at M4 Debrief close, not in this exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — module hands un-packaged to M5.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Exercise ends with 'tree settled before the Debrief's send-off' (line 87, 91). The send-off, compound, and laptop-awake instructions live in the module's Debrief section (lines 95-101) — not stolen into this exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — first experiment of a two-run arc.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 17 'The point:' names the contract verbatim ('first experiment of a two-run arc. The un-packaged send-off here teaches Module 5 what packaging adds, a lesson no lecture can land'). Three-phase exercise shape (pick / walk-and-fill / see-the-frame) matches strategy doc M4 moves 1-4.
